content.full_text:
November 4, 2008

Nancy Lockwood
The Lockwood Agency
500 North Willis
Visalia, CA 93291

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-08-182

Dear Ms. Lockwood:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1

The subject of your question, whether a committee can be organized as a nonprofit organization and qualify for a nonprofit postage rate, is not one that is addressed by the Act. We suggest that you contact the Internal Revenue Service or the Franchise Tax Board for information on the tax status granted to political committees.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance Division

CW:jgl

1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File number listed as W-08-182 (W prefix may indicate different classification). Content indicates declination of advice on tax-related matter outside FPPC jurisdiction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a political committee be organized as a nonprofit organization and qualify for a nonprofit postage rate under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined that the question of whether a committee can be organized as a nonprofit organization and qualify for a nonprofit postage rate is not addressed by the Political Reform Act, and directed the requester to contact the Internal Revenue Service or Franchise Tax Board for information on tax status granted to political committees.

We suggest that you contact the Internal Revenue Service or the Franchise Tax Board for information on the tax status granted to political committees. If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter declining to address whether a political committee can be organized as a nonprofit and qualify for nonprofit postage rates, as this matter falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.
